skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via inference.sh. Pre-installed: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, MoviePy, Pillow, OpenCV, trimesh, and 100+ more libraries. Use for: data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, 3D model processing, PDF generation, API calls, automation scripts. Triggers: python, execute code, run script, web scraping, data analysis, image processing, video editing, 3D models, automation, pandas, matplotlib
skill.summary: Run Python code in a sandboxed cloud environment with 100+ pre-installed libraries for data processing, web scraping, and automation.
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Security audit complete. Static findings flagged external commands and network access patterns, but evaluation reveals these are documentation examples in SKILL.md describing legitimate Python execution functionality. No malicious intent or user-controlled command injection vectors found. The skill documents a sandboxed Python execution service with network capabilities (HTTP requests, web scraping). Risk level set to medium due to inherent capabilities, not confirmed vulnerabilities.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3. Evaluation shows this is a false positive. The static analyzer likely misidentified the inference.sh URL pattern. No cryptographic operations are performed by this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains the skill description with inference.sh URL - no cryptographic code exists. Static scanner pattern-matching triggered incorrectly.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Network Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'network reconnaissance' at SKILL.md:163. Evaluation shows this is a false positive. Line 163 contains documentation text 'Safe execution - Runs in isolated subprocess' which has no reconnaissance behavior.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text describes sandbox isolation, not reconnaissance. Static scanner misidentified the word 'subprocess' as suspicious.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Documentation in Skill Description
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains multiple bash command examples using the belt CLI. These are documentation examples showing how to invoke the Python execution service, not user-controlled command injection. The backtick patterns flagged by static scanner are markdown code formatting, not Ruby command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static scanner detected backticks as Ruby shell commands, but they are markdown code formatting in documentation. No actual dangerous command execution patterns exist in the skill.

medium_findings.title: Network Capabilities for Python Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documents ability to make HTTP requests from Python code (using requests, httpx, beautifulsoup4). This is expected functionality for a Python executor skill. Web scraping and API integration are legitimate use cases documented in the skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is documented functionality, not a vulnerability. Users intentionally invoke Python code that may make HTTP requests. The skill describes what it does, not attempting to hide network behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs at lines 3, 11, 15, 82, 123, 182-184. These are documentation references and example URLs (example.com, inference.sh docs). No sensitive data exfiltration detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation references and example targets. No credential exfiltration or suspicious domain connections found.

content.user_title: Run Python Code in a Sandboxed Cloud Environment
content.value_statement: Need to execute Python scripts but lack local environment setup? This skill lets Claude execute Python code in a secure sandbox with 100+ pre-installed libraries, handling data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, and automation tasks without requiring local configuration.
actual_capabilities: Execute Python code in a secure, sandboxed cloud environment with CPU-only runtime
Use 100+ pre-installed libraries including pandas, numpy, matplotlib, requests, beautifulsoup4, selenium, pillow, opencv, moviepy, and trimesh
Generate and return output files (images, videos, PDFs, 3D models, CSV data) to the outputs directory
Make HTTP requests and scrape web pages using requests and beautifulsoup4
Process images (resize, crop, transform) using pillow and opencv-python-headless
Create videos with text overlays and effects using moviepy and ffmpeg
limitations: CPU-only environment with no GPU access - ML/AI models require dedicated AI apps
Execution timeout limited to 1-300 seconds (default 30 seconds)
Non-interactive execution - scripts must use plt.savefig() instead of plt.show(), and cannot rely on user input
RAM limited to 8GB by default (16GB available with high_memory variant)

use_cases.title: Data Analysis and Visualization
use_cases.description: Load CSV files, process data with pandas, and generate charts or graphs using matplotlib. Upload the data file, write processing code, and receive visualization outputs.

use_cases.title: Web Scraping and API Integration
use_cases.description: Fetch web pages, parse HTML with beautifulsoup4, and extract structured data. Make API calls to external services and process JSON responses.

use_cases.title: Media Processing and Generation
use_cases.description: Create images with PIL, generate videos with moviepy overlays, or process 3D models with trimesh. Save outputs directly to the responses directory.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and developers needing media generation

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a CSV file and create a chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Python executor to load mydata.csv, calculate average sales per region using pandas, and generate a bar chart saved to outputs/chart.png

prompt_templates.title: Scrape product information from a website
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code using requests and beautifulsoup4 to scrape product names and prices from https://example.com/products and save as outputs/products.json

prompt_templates.title: Create a video with text overlay
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video with blue background, white centered text 'Welcome' using moviepy, and save to outputs/welcome.mp4 with 24fps

prompt_templates.title: Process multiple images in batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code to resize all images in the uploads folder to 800x600 pixels using PIL, add a 10-pixel border, and save to outputs/processed/

output_examples.input: Run Python to calculate statistics for a sales dataset
output_examples.output: Loaded 1,247 records from sales.csv. Calculated: mean=$1,234.56, median=$987.00, std=$456.78. Generated histogram saved to outputs/sales_histogram.png

output_examples.input: Scrape news headlines from a website
output_examples.output: Fetched https://news.example.com, parsed 85 headlines across 12 categories. Saved structured data to outputs/headlines.json with title, category, and timestamp fields.

output_examples.input: Create a gradient image with text overlay
output_examples.output: Generated 1920x1080 gradient image (blue to purple). Added centered title 'Analysis Results' in white. Saved to outputs/gradient_banner.png

best_practices: Use plt.savefig() instead of plt.show() for visualizations since the environment is non-interactive
Specify timeout values for long-running scripts (e.g., timeout: 120 for video generation)
Save output files to the outputs/ directory so they are automatically returned with the response
anti_patterns: Do not use plt.show() - it will fail in the non-interactive sandbox environment
Avoid extremely long loops or recursive functions without timeout protection
Do not assume persistent state between executions - each run is isolated

faq.question: What Python version is supported?
faq.answer: The sandbox runs Python 3.10. NumPy, pandas, and other libraries are pre-installed with compatible versions.

faq.question: Can I install additional packages?
faq.answer: No, the environment has fixed packages. If you need a library not listed, use an alternative or request it as a skill enhancement.

faq.question: How do I handle files that are too large?
faq.answer: Use the high_memory variant (infsh/python-executor@high_memory) for datasets requiring more than 8GB RAM, or process files in chunks.

faq.question: Why did my script timeout?
faq.answer: Default timeout is 30 seconds. Increase it by setting timeout in the input (e.g., timeout: 120) or split long operations into multiple executions.

faq.question: Can I access the local filesystem?
faq.answer: Scripts can read from and write to the outputs/ directory. Upload files with --file flag and access them in your code.

faq.question: Is there GPU support for machine learning?
faq.answer: No, the environment is CPU-only. For ML/AI tasks, use dedicated AI apps that provide GPU access and pre-configured ML frameworks.
